sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew Rogers, a 14-year-old male, presented for an acne follow-up on 5/24/21.
He reported that his acne has been improving, with no redness, peeling, dryness, or irritation from the medications prescribed at his last visit on 3/3/21.
He has been using the clindamycin lotion, tretinoin cream, and 4-5% benzoyl peroxide wash on his face and 5-10% benzoyl peroxide wash on his chest and back as directed.
However, he admitted to not using the higher strength benzoyl peroxide wash on his chest and back as recommended.
He has been washing his face with the benzoyl peroxide and applying the tretinoin cream at night, and using sunscreen moisturizer.
He denied any issues with the medications and reported that his acne on his face is improving.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Clindamycin lotion
Tretinoin cream 0.025% (no dose mentioned, but implied to be 3-4% given the conversation)
4-5% benzoyl peroxide wash for face
5-10% benzoyl peroxide wash for chest and back (not used, but recommended)
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient had scattered inflammatory papules and pustules across the upper back and posterior shoulders, with less than 5 inflammatory papules on the forehead, cheeks, and chin.
Acne vulgaris is improved and well-controlled on the face, but there is a mild flare on the back.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's acne is improved and well-controlled on the face, but there is a mild flare on the back.
Problem list: Acne vulgaris; Acne vulgaris, back (mild flare)
Plan:
I recommended continuing the current treatment plan for the face and initiating treatment with PanOxyl 10% foaming wash to the back in the shower daily.
I prescribed PanOxyl 10% foaming wash for the patient to use on his chest and back.
I sent in a new refill for clindamycin with 11 refills and tretinoin cream with 3 refills.
I also provided the patient with samples and coupons for PanOxyl 10% foaming wash.
Follow-up: I scheduled a follow-up appointment for the patient in one year to monitor his acne treatment.
